name: libarchive-zip-perl
title: libarchive-zip-perl
summary: Perl module for manipulation of ZIP archives
description:
Perl module for manipulation of ZIP archives
The Archive::Zip module allows a Perl program to create, manipulate,
read, and write Zip archive files.
.
Zip archives can be created, or you can read from existing zip files.
Once created, they can be written to files, streams, or strings.
.
Members can be added, removed, extracted, replaced, rearranged, and
enumerated.  They can also be renamed or have their dates, comments,
or other attributes queried or modified.  Their data can be
compressed or uncompressed as needed.  Members can be created from
members in existing Zip files, or from existing directories, files,
or strings.

commands.name: crc32
commands.description: compute CRC-32 checksums for the given files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use crc32 because Perl module for manipulation of ZIP archives.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use crc32 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crc32 filename [ filename ... ]` as documented upstream for crc32.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair crc32 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `crc32 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libarchive-zip-perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `crc32 filename [ filename ... ]`. Perl module for manipulation of ZIP archives.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl module for manipulation of ZIP archives.
